danger_signals.visual_form: Graphical representations during real-time combat encounters on travel/battle screen
danger_signals.meaning: Indicates hazardous areas where demonic or supernatural threats exist; witches and heretics signal corruption

danger_signals.visual_form: Fortresses marked on map with occupant indicators
danger_signals.meaning: Strongholds of dangerous enemies that must be defeated to progress; visual markers of hostile territory
Environmental hazard indicating increased difficulty or resource scarcity during journey

danger_signals.visual_form: Obscure castle in hidden map location, discoverable only after defeating fortress occupants
danger_signals.meaning: Ultimate danger threshold; apocalyptic threat requiring specific world-state progression to access

safety_signals.signal: City locations in Holy Roman Empire
safety_signals.visual_form: Real historical cities marked on map in Old German names
safety_signals.meaning: Established settlements offering refuge, commerce, and quest opportunities; safer travel destinations

safety_signals.visual_form: Reputation tracking system across geographic boundaries
safety_signals.meaning: Visual reputation indicator showing acceptance in regions; high reputation signals safety and favorable NPC treatment
Sources of healing and spiritual protection; associated with safe practices and legitimate authority

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Witches and heretics appear as corrupted religious figures or anti-religious entities
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Churches, monasteries, religious quest locations in cities; presence of clerics and priests

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Monsters, witches, heretics, demon-possessed occupants in fortresses
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Fortresses harboring evil occupants; obscure castle containing Baphomet; locations of supernatural phenomena

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Historical civic identities; Old German city names
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Corrupt officials or heretical authorities within regions
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Real historical cities; regional reputation boundaries; civic quest-givers

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Fortress locations scattered across map; demonic threats contained in specific locations; normal seasonal cycles
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: NPCs give quests to hunt witches and heretics; fortresses remain as threats to confront; Baphomet location unknown

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Defeated fortresses become accessible/cleared; previously hidden castle location becomes discoverable
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Quest progression reveals final apocalyptic threat; player gains access to Baphomet's location through discovered sequence

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Baphomet's castle visually distinct; final battle arena graphics
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Demon lord encountered; apocalypse prevention mechanic triggers on defeat; game completion occurs

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Reputation tracking displays per region; NPC reaction visuals change based on local standing
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player simultaneously hated in one region and exalted in another; NPC interactions reflect regional reputation

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Travel screen visually transitions to winter aesthetic; environmental hazard indicators active
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Increased travel difficulty; reduced visibility/safety conditions; resource implications for party movement

environmental_storytelling.example: Real cities from 15th century Holy Roman Empire rendered with period Old German names; player recognizes historical context through authentic location naming and geography
Evil occupants visually mark fortresses as corrupted strongholds; discovering and defeating them reveals narrative progression toward Baphomet; fortress locations become unlocked story nodes
Churches and monasteries within cities signal established order; presence of clerics and healing practices communicate moral authority fighting against supernatural corruption
Main quest structure reveals Baphomet's location only after discovering other fortress locations; world-state unfolds through completed quest chains rather than exposition
Winter visual transition on travel screen communicates passage of time and environmental hazard; seasonal change represents world's natural threats independent of supernatural conflict

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Targeted by main quest; occupants of fortresses; represent corruption requiring elimination; quest objective markers
Provide healing services; give religious-aligned quests; communicate moral authority; safe to approach in cities
Younger characters have higher physical prowess; older characters have better starting skills; age manifests as visual/statistical degradation over time
Real-time combat encounters; must be defeated for progression; discovery triggers quest advancement
Located in obscure randomized castle; requires completion of fortress chain quests to access; final battle triggers game ending; preventing apocalypse is behavioral victory condition
Behavior changes based on player's regional reputation; can simultaneously be hostile in one region and friendly in another; visual NPC reactions reflect reputation standing

notes: Darklands uses early open-world structure with reputation mechanics that visually communicate player standing across geographically bounded regions. The game manifests world-state primarily through quest progression (fortress chain → Baphomet discovery), seasonal transitions, and reputation tracking rather than dynamic environmental changes. Supernatural vs. religious authority is the primary faction division. Combat effectiveness system (weapon vs. armor type effectiveness) communicates tactical world-state through mechanical manifestation during encounters. Nonlinear plot structure means player reputation and location-based standing serve as primary visual world-state indicators. Historical authenticity of cities and period setting grounds supernatural elements within recognizable geography.
